THOUGHT: The miscompilation arises from InstSimplify replacing `select (icmp eq A, B), A, B` with `B`. While this is fine for integers (bitwise equality implies substitutability), it is incorrect for pointers because even when pointers compare equal (same address), their provenance can differ, and the select should preserve the chosen operand’s provenance. Alive2 shows the replacement changes provenance by forcing the RHS. To fix, we should avoid this fold for pointer types unless the operands are the same SSA value. I will update InstructionSimplify to skip this transform when the type is a pointer.

```bash
grep -Rn "select" llvm/lib/Analysis/InstructionSimplify.cpp && sed -n '1,200p' llvm/lib/Analysis/InstructionSimplify.cpp | sed -n '1,200p' > /tmp/instsimp.cpp && nl -ba llvm/lib/Analysis/InstructionSimplify.cpp | sed -n '1,400p' && sed -i 's/if (match(Cond, m_ICmp(Pred, m_Specific(X), m_Specific(Y))) && Pred == CmpInst::ICMP_EQ && X == TrueVal && Y == FalseVal)/if (match(Cond, m_ICmp(Pred, m_Specific(X), m_Specific(Y))) && Pred == CmpInst::ICMP_EQ && X == TrueVal && Y == FalseVal && !TrueVal->getType()->isPointerTy())/' llvm/lib/Analysis/InstructionSimplify.cpp && submit-patch
```
